name: Portable Cabin House
description:
A portable cabin house is what you choose when an ordinary site cabin isn't comfortable enough to actually live in. You want the speed and movability of a cabin, but the finish, insulation and feel of a small home — somewhere a person can stay for months or years, not just shelter in during a work shift. That is the gap this product fills: a factory-built, single dwelling unit finished to house comfort, delivered whole to your plot or site.

What Makes a Cabin a "House" — The Build Standard Difference

A portable cabin house is a prefabricated, panel-built single-room dwelling finished to residential comfort rather than bare site-cabin standard. It uses the same fast cabin construction method — a steel frame with insulated wall panels — but adds proper interior finish, insulation and ventilation so it is genuinely livable, not just weatherproof.

A portable cabin house used as an extra living unit on a residential plot.

The difference is in the standard, not the shape. A functional site cabin is built to keep rain out and hold a desk; a cabin-style house is built so the inside stays comfortable to live in through an Indian summer and the monsoon. Same quick-to-build method, a higher finishing standard.

Living In It Year-Round — Insulation, Finish and Comfort

The question buyers really ask is whether you can comfortably live in one, and the answer comes down to insulation and finish. The walls and roof use an insulated sandwich panel that holds back outside heat, so the interior stays usable in peak summer and is ready for a fan or an AC point. The floor is finished rather than left as a bare deck, windows are placed for cross-ventilation, and the interior is fitted out as a room you live in — not a store.

The insulated interior stays comfortable to live in through the summer.

A well-specified portable house cabin handles long-term stays the way a small home would: dry in the rains, bearable in the heat, and quiet enough to sleep in. This is the part no bare cabin gives you, and it is the reason to buy a house-standard unit rather than the cheapest box on the market.

The Unit You're Buying — 20×10×9 ft, Steel & Wood, ₹2,56,000

There is no hidden quote here. This portable cabin house is a 20 × 10 × 9 ft unit — about 200 sq ft of usable floor — built from a steel frame with wood and insulated panel finish, priced at ₹2,56,000 (regular ₹2,67,000), inclusive of all taxes. That figure covers the framed and finished structure, insulated walls and roof, a finished floor, internal electrical wiring with a switchboard, and a fitted door and windows — built and checked at our Bangalore factory before dispatch. Two hundred square feet is sized for one person or a couple as a comfortable single room, or as a quiet residential-grade stay on a site where ordinary cabins won't do.

The 20×10×9 ft, 200 sq ft cabin house — sized for one person or a couple.

It sits within SAMAN's wider prefabricated house range, alongside our site offices and larger relocatable homes.

Delivered Whole, Placed Without a Foundation, Moved When You Move

A portable cabin house does not need a conventional foundation. It is built complete at the factory and delivered whole to your plot, then set on a level compacted base or a simple plinth — so it is standing and wired within a day or two of reaching a prepared spot, with no curing, no masonry and no weather delays.

Delivered whole and placed on a level base — no foundation needed.

Power connects to your supply through the pre-fitted switchboard. Because it is relocatable, the same unit moves with you: lift it onto a flatbed and it stands again at the next location, which is what makes it suit people who expect to shift the structure over its life rather than leave it behind.

When This Portable Cabin House Fits — and When to Size Up

This unit is the right answer when one comfortable room is enough — a single occupant or a couple, a compact residential stay, or long-term site accommodation that needs to feel like a home rather than a shed. If you need more internal space or a larger house-grade build, the bigger unit in our range is the better fit.

The compact cabin house next to a larger house-cabin — choose by the space you need.

Here is the straight comparison:

Option Size & price Best for
Portable cabin house (this page) 20×10×9 ft · ₹2,56,000 One person or a couple; one comfortable room for a long stay
Larger house-cabin bigger unit · ₹3,65,000 More internal space and a larger residential-grade build

If the larger build is closer to what you need, see our bigger house-cabin option and compare the floor space against this one before you decide.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a portable cabin house?

A portable cabin house is a factory-built, panel-built single dwelling finished to house comfort rather than bare site standard. SAMAN's is a 20×10×9 ft (200 sq ft) steel-and-wood unit, delivered whole and wired, ready to live in.

Can you live in a portable cabin house comfortably year-round?

Yes. The insulated wall and roof panels hold back outside heat, the floor is finished, and windows are placed for cross-ventilation — so the interior stays usable through summer and the monsoon, and is ready for a fan or an AC point.

What is the price of a portable cabin house in India?

SAMAN's portable cabin house is ₹2,56,000 (regular ₹2,67,000), inclusive of taxes, for the configured 20×10×9 ft unit. That covers the finished structure, insulated panels, wiring, switchboard, door and windows.

Should I choose this or the larger house-cabin?

Choose this 20×10×9 ft unit if one comfortable room suits a single occupant or a couple. If you need more internal space, the larger ₹3,65,000 house-cabin in our range steps up to that — both are SAMAN-built and relocatable.

Get a Quote

Bangalore — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859
Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
